---
title: "Education and Political Approval"
author: "Alec Rogers"
format: html
execute: 
  echo: false
---


```{r}
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)
library(primer.data)

```

Political ideology relates to presidential approval. We use 2020 data from the CES survey to explore this relationship among American voters.Since our data come from only 2020 and from a survey, applicability of our findings is limited to other time periods and populations that may not respond to political surveys.


```{r}
# create the data set
x <- ces |>
filter(year == 2020) |>
dplyr::select(approval, education, ideology) |>
drop_na() |>
slice_sample(n = 2000)

```


```{r}
x |>
  ggplot(aes(x = ideology, fill = approval)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(
    title = "Approval by Political Ideology",
    subtitle = "Distribution of  presidential approval across ideological groups in 2020",
    x = "Ideology",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Approval",
    caption = "Source: ces dataset, 2020 responses"
  )

```
